atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream diff for crates/primitives-traits/src/transaction/error.rs introduces behavior or wiring that must be accounted for in the v2.2 merge target.

destinations.evidence: The executor layer carries a dedicated permanent ONNX/precompile request validation error even though v2.2 consumes upstream reth-primitives-traits rather than the old local enum variant.
The payload builder now maps permanent precompile validation errors to the existing hard-invalid pool path, preserving eviction of the transaction and descendants.
The replacement hard-invalid consensus error is still classified as a bad transaction for pool eviction/descendant invalidation semantics.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The old local `InvalidTransactionError::PrecompileRequestPermanentlyInvalid` variant is no longer available because v2.2 consumes upstream `reth-primitives-traits`. The behavior is preserved by keeping the specific permanent-precompile signal in Alloy EVM and mapping it at Reth payload/pending-block boundaries to the existing hard-invalid `TxTypeNotSupported` pool error, which still evicts the transaction and descendants.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Trigger an ONNX malformed-request sentinel during payload building and assert the builder marks the pool transaction invalid instead of returning a fatal payload error.
atoms.notes: Restored at the payload and pending-block error boundary using the v2.2 upstream transaction-error surface.

summary: Permanent precompile request eviction is preserved through Alloy EVM validation errors and the v2.2 hard-invalid pool classification path.
notes: Reclassified from missing after restoring the Reth payload/pending-block mapping for permanent precompile validation errors.
